enrichment.short_description: VictoriaMetrics is a fast, cost-efficient, open-source time series database and monitoring solution that is API-compatible with Prometheus, built for high-cardinality metrics at scale.
enrichment.seo_title: VictoriaMetrics Review: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: VictoriaMetrics is an open-source, Prometheus-compatible time series database for monitoring at scale. See pricing, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: VictoriaMetrics is an open-source, high-performance time series database and monitoring platform originally created in 2018 by a team of engineers (including Roman Khavronenko and Aliaksandr Valialkin) who had previously built monitoring systems at companies like Lucene/Wix; the company is now headquartered in San Francisco with roots tracing back to Kyiv, Ukraine, and has grown without external venture funding, remaining largely bootstrapped. It is designed as a drop-in replacement for Prometheus and Graphite, offering long-term storage, PromQL and MetricsQL query support, high compression, and the ability to scale from a single binary on a laptop to large distributed clusters ingesting billions of data points. The product family has expanded beyond metrics into VictoriaLogs (log management) and VictoriaTraces (distributed tracing), positioning VictoriaMetrics as a broader observability suite. It is used by organizations such as CERN, Grammarly, Spotify R&D, Wix, Adidas, and Cornell University, valued for its low resource footprint and operational simplicity compared to alternatives like Prometheus with Thanos/Cortex/Mimir, InfluxDB, or Datadog. The tool is offered three ways: a free, community-supported open-source version under Apache 2.0-style licensing; an Enterprise edition adding features like downsampling, multi-tenancy, retention filters, and vendor support; and VictoriaMetrics Cloud, a fully managed SaaS offering with fixed-tier compute and storage pricing starting around $190 per month for the smallest tiers, plus $0.09/GB egress, and a $200 trial credit for new accounts. Compared to Prometheus-ecosystem alternatives, VictoriaMetrics is frequently cited in benchmarks for lower RAM/disk usage and faster ingestion at scale, making it a common choice for teams outgrowing raw Prometheus but wanting to avoid the operational complexity of Thanos, Cortex, or Mimir.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is VictoriaMetrics
paragraphs: VictoriaMetrics is an open-source time series database purpose-built for storing and querying metrics at scale. It is compatible with the Prometheus remote write and query APIs, so it can slot into existing Prometheus-based monitoring stacks as a long-term storage backend or full replacement without requiring teams to rewrite their alerting rules or dashboards.

The project emphasizes operational simplicity: it ships as a single, dependency-free binary that can run on a laptop for personal projects or be deployed as a distributed cluster handling billions of metrics per day for large enterprises. Its design goals of high compression, fast ingestion, and low RAM/CPU usage have made it a popular alternative to Prometheus plus Thanos, Cortex, or Mimir for teams that need long-term retention without heavy infrastructure overhead.

Beyond metrics, VictoriaMetrics has expanded into a broader observability suite with VictoriaLogs for log management and VictoriaTraces for distributed tracing, letting teams consolidate metrics, logs, and traces around a similar storage engine and query philosophy.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: VictoriaMetrics supports PromQL and its own extended MetricsQL query language, high data compression that reduces storage costs versus Prometheus and InfluxDB, and native support for OpenTelemetry, Kubernetes, and Grafana integrations.

It scales from a single-node instance to a highly available cluster mode with independent scaling of ingestion, storage, and query components, and includes enterprise add-ons such as downsampling, multi-tenancy, and retention filtering for larger organizations.
The platform also offers AI-assisted anomaly detection features and a managed Cloud offering that removes the operational burden of running and scaling the database in-house.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: VictoriaMetrics is free and open source under a permissive license, and most users can self-host it at no licensing cost. For organizations wanting vendor support and advanced features, an Enterprise license is available with custom pricing.

VictoriaMetrics Cloud, the fully managed SaaS version, uses a fixed-tier pricing model combining compute and storage costs, with entry-level tiers starting around $190 per month plus $0.09 per GB of network egress; new accounts receive a time-limited trial credit and no credit card is required to start.

features.title: Prometheus-compatible API
features.description: Drop-in remote write and PromQL query compatibility with existing Prometheus deployments and Grafana dashboards.

features.title: High compression storage engine
features.description: Custom storage format that significantly reduces disk usage compared to Prometheus, InfluxDB, and other time series databases.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Runs as one dependency-free binary for simple installs, from laptops to production servers.

features.title: Cluster mode for scale
features.description: Independently scalable ingestion, storage, and query components for handling billions of time series.

features.title: MetricsQL
features.description: An extended, backward-compatible superset of PromQL with additional functions for advanced querying.

features.title: VictoriaLogs
features.description: A companion log management system built on similar storage principles for unified observability.

features.title: VictoriaTraces
features.description: A distributed tracing solution extending the observability suite beyond metrics and logs.

features.title: OpenTelemetry and Kubernetes support
features.description: Native ingestion of OpenTelemetry data and integrations for monitoring Kubernetes clusters.

pros: Free and open source with a large, active community and frequent releases
Significantly lower RAM, CPU, and disk usage than many alternatives at similar scale
Drop-in compatibility with existing Prometheus and Grafana tooling
Scales from single-node to large distributed clusters without switching tools
Managed Cloud option removes operational overhead for teams that want it
cons: Cloud pricing model can be harder to estimate than simple per-seat SaaS pricing
Enterprise features require a paid license, gating some advanced capabilities
Steeper learning curve for teams unfamiliar with Prometheus-style monitoring concepts
Cluster mode setup and tuning requires infrastructure expertise for very large deployments

faqs.question: Is VictoriaMetrics free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core VictoriaMetrics database is free and open source. Enterprise and Cloud offerings are paid options for teams that want extra features or managed hosting.

faqs.question: Is VictoriaMetrics compatible with Prometheus?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports the Prometheus remote write protocol and PromQL, so it can be used as a long-term storage backend or full replacement for Prometheus.

faqs.question: What is VictoriaMetrics Cloud?
faqs.answer: VictoriaMetrics Cloud is the company's fully managed SaaS offering, handling deployment, scaling, and backups, with pricing starting around $190 per month.

faqs.question: Who founded VictoriaMetrics?
faqs.answer: VictoriaMetrics was founded in 2018 by engineers with prior experience building large-scale monitoring systems, and it has grown as a largely bootstrapped company.

faqs.question: What is the difference between VictoriaMetrics and VictoriaLogs?
faqs.answer: VictoriaMetrics stores and queries numeric time series (metrics), while VictoriaLogs is a companion product for storing and searching log data using a similar storage philosophy.

faqs.question: Can VictoriaMetrics replace Thanos, Cortex, or Mimir?
faqs.answer: Many teams use VictoriaMetrics as a simpler alternative to these long-term storage solutions for Prometheus, citing lower resource usage and easier operations.

faqs.question: Does VictoriaMetrics support Grafana?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates directly with Grafana as a data source using its Prometheus-compatible query API.

faqs.question: Who uses VictoriaMetrics in production?
faqs.answer: Organizations including CERN, Grammarly, Spotify R&D, Wix, Adidas, and Cornell University have publicly discussed using VictoriaMetrics.

use_cases.title: Long-term Prometheus metrics storage
use_cases.description: Teams outgrowing local Prometheus retention use VictoriaMetrics as scalable, cost-efficient long-term storage.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes and cloud-native observability
use_cases.description: Monitoring large, dynamic Kubernetes environments with high-cardinality metrics and OpenTelemetry data.

use_cases.title: Managed monitoring without ops overhead
use_cases.description: Organizations that want Prometheus-style monitoring without running and scaling the database themselves use VictoriaMetrics Cloud.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams without dedicated database operators

enrichment.short_description: VLC media player is a free, open-source, cross-platform application that plays nearly any audio or video file, disc, or stream, developed by the nonprofit VideoLAN organization.
enrichment.company_size: Volunteer nonprofit (small core team plus community contributors)
enrichment.seo_title: VLC Media Player Review: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: VLC (VideoLAN Client) is the free, open-source cross-platform media player. Learn its history, features, and how it compares to alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: VLC media player, formally VideoLAN Client, is a free and open-source, cross-platform multimedia player and framework capable of playing almost any video or audio file, DVD, Audio CD, VCD, and streaming protocol without needing extra codec packs. The project traces its roots to 1996 as a student project at the Ecole Centrale Paris engineering school (originally called VideoLAN Client, part of a campus video-streaming initiative), and became open source in 2001 when the university released it under the GPL, allowing outside contributors to port it to Windows and macOS. Developer Jean-Baptiste Kempf joined the project in 2003 and took over leadership around 2006, rebuilding the codebase and community. On April 21, 2009, VideoLAN was formally established as a nonprofit association under French law (association loi 1901) to give the project legal structure independent of the university; Kempf serves as its president. VLC is entirely free, funded by user donations, and carries no ads, no spyware, and no user tracking, which the organization and Kempf have publicly emphasized by declining lucrative acquisition and bundling offers over the years. It runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, and other platforms, and beyond the player itself, VideoLAN maintains related open-source projects including the x264 and x265 video encoders and the libVLC library used by other software. VLC has no direct commercial pricing or business model since it is entirely free and open source, distinguishing it from freemium or subscription-based media tools and cementing its reputation as one of the most widely used media players in the world, with well over 3 billion downloads historically.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is VideoLAN Client (VLC)
paragraphs: VLC is a free, open-source, cross-platform multimedia player and framework that plays most audio and video formats, discs, devices, and streams without requiring additional codec installations. It is developed and maintained by VideoLAN, a nonprofit organization under French law.

The project began in 1996 as a student initiative at the Ecole Centrale Paris engineering school to build a video-streaming system for the campus network. It was released as open-source software under the GPL in 2001, which allowed outside developers to contribute and quickly led to ports for Windows and macOS.

Developer Jean-Baptiste Kempf joined the project in 2003 and took over its leadership around 2006, and VideoLAN was formally incorporated as a nonprofit association on April 21, 2009, giving the project a legal and financial structure funded by user donations rather than advertising or data collection.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: VLC plays nearly all video and audio codecs out of the box, including MPEG-2, MPEG-4, H.264, H.265, MKV, and many others, without needing separate codec packs.
It supports playback of DVDs, Audio CDs, VCDs, and various streaming protocols, along with network streaming, format conversion, and screen recording capabilities.
VLC is available on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS, and its underlying libVLC library is used by many other applications and devices.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: VLC is entirely free to download and use, with no premium tier, subscription, or in-app purchases. VideoLAN funds its operations through user donations, and the organization has repeatedly turned down offers to monetize VLC through ads or bundled software.

features.title: Universal codec support
features.description: Plays nearly any audio or video format without needing to install separate codec packs.

features.title: Cross-platform availability
features.description: Native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS.

features.title: Disc and device playback
features.description: Supports DVDs, Audio CDs, VCDs, and various external devices and capture cards.

features.title: Network streaming
features.description: Can stream media over networks and play a wide range of internet streaming protocols.

features.title: Format conversion
features.description: Built-in tools to convert media between formats and encode video/audio.

features.title: No ads or tracking
features.description: Free of advertising, spyware, and user tracking as a matter of organizational policy.

features.title: Customizable via extensions
features.description: Supports skins, plugins, and extensions to customize playback and interface.

features.title: Open-source codebase
features.description: Fully open source under the GPL, allowing community auditing and contribution.

pros: Completely free with no ads, subscriptions, or tracking
Plays virtually any media format without extra codecs
Available on nearly every major desktop and mobile platform
Backed by a transparent nonprofit organization funded by donations
Long track record of active development and community trust
cons: Interface feels dated compared to some modern media players
Lacks built-in streaming service integrations found in commercial apps
Advanced features can be less discoverable for casual users
Relies on volunteer and donation-based funding, which can limit resources

faqs.question: Is VLC media player free?
faqs.answer: Yes, VLC is completely free to download and use on all supported platforms, with no premium version or subscription.

faqs.question: Who develops VLC?
faqs.answer: VLC is developed by VideoLAN, a nonprofit organization under French law, led by Jean-Baptiste Kempf.

faqs.question: When was VLC created?
faqs.answer: The underlying project began in 1996 as a student project at Ecole Centrale Paris and was released as open-source software in 2001.

faqs.question: What platforms does VLC support?
faqs.answer: VLC runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS, among other systems.

faqs.question: Does VLC contain ads or spyware?
faqs.answer: No, VLC is explicitly free of advertising, spyware, and user tracking as a core project principle.

faqs.question: Can VLC play DVDs and other discs?
faqs.answer: Yes, VLC can play DVDs, Audio CDs, VCDs, and various other disc formats natively.

faqs.question: Is VideoLAN a company?
faqs.answer: No, VideoLAN is a nonprofit association formed under French law in 2009, funded by donations rather than being a commercial company.

faqs.question: What other software does VideoLAN maintain?
faqs.answer: Beyond VLC, VideoLAN develops projects like the x264 and x265 video encoders and the libVLC library used by other applications.

use_cases.title: Universal media playback
use_cases.description: Playing virtually any downloaded or local video or audio file without worrying about missing codecs.

use_cases.title: Streaming and network playback
use_cases.description: Streaming media over local networks or the internet using VLC's built-in streaming and casting tools.

use_cases.title: Lightweight media conversion
use_cases.description: Converting or transcoding video and audio files between formats using VLC's built-in converter.

enrichment.short_description: VidIQ is an AI-powered YouTube growth platform that helps creators research keywords, optimize titles and thumbnails, track trends, and analyze channel performance.
enrichment.seo_title: VidIQ Review: Pricing, Features and Alternatives for YouTube
enrichment.seo_meta_description: VidIQ is an AI-powered YouTube SEO and analytics tool for creators. See pricing plans, features, pros, cons, and how it compares to alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: VidIQ is an AI-powered YouTube channel growth and optimization platform founded in 2011 (as a Chrome extension analytics company) by Rob Sandie and Todd Troxell, originally based in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, before relocating its headquarters to San Francisco, California. In its early history the company raised $800,000 from investors including Mark Cuban in 2013 and achieved official YouTube certification the same year. Today VidIQ serves millions of creators across more than 190 countries through a browser extension, mobile apps, and web dashboard that provide keyword research, competitor analysis, trend discovery, AI-generated title, tag, and thumbnail suggestions, and channel analytics. The product uses an AI-credit system across its paid tiers, offering a free plan with 150 monthly AI credits, a Boost plan aimed at growing creators, a Max plan at $39 per month (about $16.58/month billed annually) with 6,000 AI credits and expanded AI coaching and clipping tools, and a custom-priced Enterprise plan for brands, agencies, and multi-channel networks needing team roles and cross-channel reporting. VidIQ positions itself against competitors like TubeBuddy (a similar YouTube extension-based optimization tool) and native YouTube Studio analytics, differentiating through its AI Coach feature, trend prediction, and creator coaching services. The company also runs YouTube-focused educational content and a Top 100 channel tracker. With roughly 51-200 employees and a long operating history in the creator economy space, VidIQ is one of the more established third-party tools built specifically around YouTube's algorithm and creator monetization ecosystem, alongside video editing and thumbnail tools that address adjacent parts of a creator's workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is VidIQ
paragraphs: VidIQ is a YouTube-focused growth and optimization platform that gives creators data and AI-driven suggestions to help videos perform better in search and recommendations. It is delivered through a browser extension, web dashboard, and mobile apps.

Founded in 2011 by Rob Sandie and Todd Troxell, VidIQ started as a Chrome extension providing analytics overlays directly on YouTube and has since grown into a broader suite covering keyword research, trend discovery, competitor tracking, and AI content generation for titles, descriptions, tags, and thumbnails.

The company relocated its headquarters to San Francisco and now serves millions of creators worldwide, positioning itself alongside tools like TubeBuddy as one of the leading third-party platforms built specifically for the YouTube creator ecosystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: VidIQ's browser extension overlays real-time keyword scores, competitor tags, and video statistics directly on YouTube pages, while its dashboard provides deeper trend research and channel audits.
AI features generate title, description, and tag suggestions, create thumbnail concepts, and power an AI Coach that answers creator questions about growth strategy and channel performance.
The platform also includes subscriber and audience insight tools, competitive channel tracking, and short-form clipping tools for turning long videos into shorts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: VidIQ offers a free plan with limited monthly AI credits and basic trend research. Paid tiers include a Boost plan for growing creators and a Max plan priced at $39 per month billed monthly, or roughly $16.58 per month when billed annually, which unlocks more AI credits, expanded AI Coach access, and clipping tools.
An Enterprise plan with custom pricing is available for brands, agencies, and multi-channel networks that need team permissions, priority support, and cross-channel reporting.

features.title: Keyword and SEO research
features.description: Real-time keyword scoring and search volume data tailored to YouTube's search algorithm.

features.title: AI title and description generator
features.description: AI-generated suggestions for video titles, descriptions, and tags to improve discoverability.

features.title: Thumbnail generation and testing
features.description: AI-assisted thumbnail creation and tools to compare thumbnail performance.

features.title: Competitor tracking
features.description: Monitors competing channels' keywords, upload patterns, and performance trends.

features.title: Trend discovery
features.description: Surfaces rising topics and search trends relevant to a creator's niche.

features.title: AI Coach
features.description: A conversational AI assistant offering channel growth and strategy advice.

features.title: Browser extension overlay
features.description: Displays keyword and competitor data directly within the YouTube interface.

features.title: Channel analytics dashboard
features.description: Tracks subscriber growth, video performance, and audience retention metrics.

pros: Deep, YouTube-specific keyword and SEO data not available in native YouTube Studio
Free plan provides real value for smaller or new creators
AI tools reduce time spent on titles, tags, and thumbnails
Long operating history and large, active creator community
Enterprise tier supports agencies and multi-channel management
cons: AI-credit system on paid plans can feel limiting for heavy users
Some advanced features are locked behind the higher-priced Max tier
Primarily focused on YouTube, with limited support for other platforms
Browser extension can slow down YouTube page load for some users

faqs.question: Is VidIQ free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, VidIQ offers a free plan with limited AI credits and core keyword and trend research tools; paid plans unlock more AI usage and advanced features.

faqs.question: How much does VidIQ Max cost?
faqs.answer: VidIQ Max costs $39 per month billed monthly, or about $16.58 per month when billed annually.

faqs.question: Who founded VidIQ?
faqs.answer: VidIQ was founded in 2011 by Rob Sandie and Todd Troxell.

faqs.question: Where is VidIQ headquartered?
faqs.answer: VidIQ is headquartered in San Francisco, California, having originally started in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania.

faqs.question: What is VidIQ's AI Coach?
faqs.answer: AI Coach is a conversational assistant within VidIQ that answers questions about channel strategy, video performance, and growth tactics.

faqs.question: Does VidIQ have an Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, VidIQ offers a custom-priced Enterprise plan for brands, agencies, and multi-channel networks managing multiple YouTube channels.

faqs.question: Is VidIQ similar to TubeBuddy?
faqs.answer: Yes, VidIQ and TubeBuddy are the two best-known third-party browser extension tools for YouTube SEO and channel optimization.

faqs.question: Does VidIQ work on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, VidIQ offers mobile apps for iOS and Android in addition to its browser extension and web dashboard.

use_cases.title: YouTube keyword research

use_cases.title: Channel growth coaching
use_cases.description: Using AI Coach and analytics to identify what content and formats drive subscriber growth.

use_cases.title: Multi-channel agency management
use_cases.description: Managing SEO and reporting across many client or brand YouTube channels from one account.

features: Up to 11 hours of short clipping

enrichment.short_description: Vidnoz is an AI-powered video creation platform offering AI avatars, text-to-video, voice cloning, and video translation tools for marketers, educators, and content creators.
languages: and 140+ languages for AI voice/video output
enrichment.seo_title: Vidnoz Review: AI Video Generator Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vidnoz is an AI video generation platform with avatars, voice cloning, and translation tools. Compare pricing, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Vidnoz is an AI-powered video creation and editing platform founded in 2016 and headquartered in Los Angeles, United States, built around a large library of AI avatars, AI voices, and templated video tools aimed at marketers, educators, sales teams, and individual content creators. The platform provides more than 1,900 realistic AI avatars and over 2,000 AI voices supporting 140-plus languages, along with voice cloning, text-to-speech, image-to-video conversion, video translation with lip-syncing, AI image generation and editing, and background removal, delivered through thousands of pre-built templates. Vidnoz follows a freemium model: the free tier grants daily credits (commonly cited as around 60 credits per day) that allow limited-length videos at capped resolution with a watermark, while paid plans, such as a Starter tier around $14.99 to $19.99 per month when billed annually, remove watermarks, unlock 1080p exports, and add more monthly credits; a Business tier adds team collaboration and voice cloning at scale. The company states it is ISO/IEC 27001:2022 compliant and is used by more than 1,000 media and business organizations. Vidnoz competes in the increasingly crowded AI avatar and synthetic video space alongside tools like Synthesia, HeyGen, and D-ID, differentiating primarily through its very large free-tier credit allowance and broad template library rather than premium enterprise features. Because Vidnoz spans many narrower sub-tools (video translator, AI avatar generator, image generator, video editor), pricing can vary by specific product page, and prospective users should check the current plan they need directly on vidnoz.com before subscribing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Vidnoz
paragraphs: Vidnoz is an AI video creation platform that combines AI avatars, voice generation, and templated editing tools to let users produce videos quickly without filming or professional editing skills. It targets marketers, educators, HR and sales teams, and individual creators.

Founded in 2016 and based in Los Angeles, the company has built out a large catalog of over 1,900 AI avatars and 2,000-plus AI voices covering more than 140 languages, positioning itself as an accessible, budget-friendly alternative to enterprise AI video tools.

Vidnoz's product suite spans multiple specialized tools, including an AI avatar video generator, a video translator with lip-sync, an AI image generator, and background removal and photo editing utilities, all accessible from a unified account.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include AI avatars with natural gestures, text-to-speech and voice cloning, image-to-video conversion, and thousands of customizable video templates.

Video translation with automatic lip-syncing lets users localize existing videos into other languages, while AI image generation and editing tools extend the platform beyond video into static content creation.
Vidnoz also supports music video generation, video dubbing, and script generation, aiming to cover most steps of the video production workflow in one platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Vidnoz offers a free plan with daily AI credits that supports short, watermarked videos at limited resolution. Paid plans start with a Starter tier priced roughly $14.99 to $19.99 per month when billed annually, which removes watermarks and enables 1080p exports with more monthly credits.

A Business plan adds features like voice cloning at scale and team collaboration; pricing for higher tiers and specific sub-tools (such as the video translator) varies and is listed on dedicated pricing pages on vidnoz.com.

features.title: AI avatars
features.description: Over 1,900 realistic digital avatars with natural gestures and expressions for presenter-style videos.

features.title: AI voice generation and cloning
features.description: More than 2,000 AI voices across 140-plus languages, plus custom voice cloning.

features.title: Video translation with lip-sync
features.description: Translates existing videos into other languages while syncing the speaker's lip movements.

features.title: Text-to-video and image-to-video
features.description: Converts scripts or static images into produced video content automatically.

features.title: Template library
features.description: Over 2,800 pre-designed templates for marketing, education, and social media videos.

features.title: AI image generation and editing
features.description: Generates and edits images, including background removal, alongside the video toolset.

features.title: Video dubbing and script generation
features.description: Tools to dub existing content and auto-generate scripts for new videos.

features.title: Music video generator
features.description: Creates music-synced videos from AI-generated or uploaded audio tracks.

pros: Generous free tier with daily credits for casual use
Very large avatar, voice, and template library
Broad multilingual support for voices and translation
Covers many video and image tasks in a single platform
Budget-friendly compared to enterprise AI video tools
cons: Free tier videos are capped in length and resolution with a watermark
Pricing structure spans many sub-tools, which can be confusing to compare
AI avatar realism can vary by avatar compared to top-tier competitors
Best higher-end features gated behind Business-level plans

faqs.question: Is Vidnoz free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vidnoz offers a free plan with daily AI credits, though videos are limited in length, capped at lower resolution, and carry a watermark.

faqs.question: How much does Vidnoz cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans typically start around $14.99 to $19.99 per month when billed annually, depending on the specific tool and tier chosen.

faqs.question: When was Vidnoz founded?
faqs.answer: Vidnoz was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Los Angeles, United States.

faqs.question: How many AI avatars does Vidnoz offer?
faqs.answer: Vidnoz offers more than 1,900 AI avatars along with over 2,000 AI voices across 140-plus languages.

faqs.question: Can Vidnoz translate videos into other languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vidnoz includes a video translation feature with automatic lip-syncing for localizing existing videos.

faqs.question: Does Vidnoz support voice cloning?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vidnoz offers AI voice cloning to replicate a user's own voice for narration.

faqs.question: Who is Vidnoz best suited for?
faqs.answer: Vidnoz is aimed at marketers, educators, sales teams, and individual creators who need quick, low-cost AI-generated video content.

faqs.question: Is Vidnoz secure and compliant?
faqs.answer: Vidnoz states it maintains ISO/IEC 27001:2022 compliance and publishes a transparent privacy policy.

enrichment.short_description: Vikunja is a free, open-source, self-hostable to-do list and project management app offering list, Kanban, Gantt, and table views for individuals and teams.
enrichment.seo_title: Vikunja Review: Open-Source To-Do App Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vikunja is a free, open-source, self-hostable to-do and project management app. Compare features, pricing, and alternatives like Todoist and Trello.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Vikunja is an open-source, self-hostable to-do list and project management application whose first code commit dates to June 2018, created by a developer known as kolaente (Konrad). It was built to give users full ownership of their task data as a privacy-respecting alternative to proprietary task managers such as Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do, and it includes built-in migration tools to import data directly from those platforms. Vikunja offers four ways to view work: list, Kanban board, Gantt chart, and table views, alongside features for sharing projects, assigning tasks, setting reminders, and organizing personal or team workloads. The software is licensed under AGPLv3 and can be self-hosted for free on a user's own server or infrastructure, giving full control over data. For users who prefer not to self-host, the company offers Vikunja Cloud, a paid managed hosting service described as 'made and hosted in the EU' and run as a bootstrapped business without external venture funding; Cloud plans include a Personal plan at 4 euros per month (or 40 euros per year) with 10GB of storage, a Family plan at 10 euros per month for up to five users, and an Organization plan at 5 euros per user per month with 50GB storage and priority support, all backed by a 14-day free trial. Vikunja is positioned in the growing category of open-source SaaS alternatives, similar in spirit to tools like Nextcloud Tasks, competing on transparency, data ownership, and lack of vendor lock-in rather than enterprise feature breadth, appealing especially to privacy-conscious individuals, small teams, and self-hosting enthusiasts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Vikunja
paragraphs: Vikunja is an open-source to-do list and project management application designed to give users complete ownership over their task data, either by self-hosting the software themselves or subscribing to the official managed Cloud service.

Development began with a first commit in June 2018, created because the founder found no existing open-source tool that met their needs for a privacy-respecting, self-hostable task manager. The project has since grown into a mature application supporting personal and team use.
Vikunja includes migration tools for users switching from Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do, making it straightforward to move existing task lists and projects into the platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The app supports four distinct views of the same underlying tasks: a simple list, a Kanban board, a Gantt chart for timeline planning, and a table view for spreadsheet-style organization.
Users can share projects, assign tasks to teammates, set due dates and reminders, and collaborate without needing to switch to a separate tool for team coordination.
Because the entire codebase is open source under AGPLv3, users and organizations can audit the code, self-host it on their own infrastructure, and even modify it to fit specific needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Vikunja is completely free when self-hosted, since the software is open source. For users who want managed hosting, Vikunja Cloud offers a Personal plan at 4 euros per month, a Family plan at 10 euros per month for up to five users, and an Organization plan at 5 euros per user per month with more storage and priority support.
All Vikunja Cloud plans include a 14-day free trial, and the company describes itself as bootstrapped and EU-hosted, without outside venture capital influencing its roadmap.

features.title: Multiple project views
features.description: List, Kanban, Gantt, and table views of the same tasks for different planning styles.

features.title: Self-hosting option
features.description: Fully open-source AGPLv3 codebase that can be deployed on a user's own server.

features.title: Managed Cloud hosting
features.description: Paid, EU-hosted managed service for users who don't want to self-host.

features.title: Task sharing and assignment
features.description: Share projects and assign tasks to collaborators for team-based work.

features.title: Import from other tools
features.description: Built-in migration from Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do.

features.title: Reminders and due dates
features.description: Task reminders and deadline tracking to keep work on schedule.

features.title: Unlimited lists and tasks
features.description: No artificial caps on the number of projects, tasks, or reminders on Cloud plans.

features.title: Cross-platform access
features.description: Web app plus mobile and desktop clients for accessing tasks anywhere.

pros: Completely free and open source when self-hosted
Full data ownership and privacy control
Multiple flexible views (list, Kanban, Gantt, table) in one app
Affordable managed Cloud option for non-technical users
Active open-source community and transparent development
cons: Self-hosting requires technical setup and maintenance
Smaller ecosystem of integrations compared to major commercial task managers
UI and polish can lag behind well-funded competitors like Todoist
Support relies mainly on community channels for self-hosted users

faqs.question: Is Vikunja free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vikunja is free and open source when self-hosted. A paid Vikunja Cloud option is available for users who prefer managed hosting.

faqs.question: How much does Vikunja Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Vikunja Cloud starts at 4 euros per month for the Personal plan, with Family and Organization plans priced at 10 euros per month and 5 euros per user per month respectively.

faqs.question: Can I migrate from Todoist or Trello to Vikunja?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vikunja includes built-in migration tools to import tasks and projects from Todoist, Trello, and Microsoft To Do.

faqs.question: Is Vikunja open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vikunja is licensed under AGPLv3 and its full source code is publicly available.

faqs.question: When did Vikunja start?
faqs.answer: Vikunja's development began with a first commit in June 2018.

faqs.question: What views does Vikunja support?
faqs.answer: Vikunja supports list, Kanban, Gantt chart, and table views of your tasks and projects.

faqs.question: Can teams use Vikunja together?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vikunja supports project sharing and task assignment for team collaboration.

faqs.question: Where is Vikunja Cloud hosted?
faqs.answer: Vikunja Cloud is made and hosted in the European Union.

enrichment.short_description: Vimeo Events is Vimeo's virtual and hybrid events product for hosting live streams, webinars, and always-on broadcasts with engagement and lead-capture tools.
enrichment.company_size: 51-200 employees (Vimeo Events team, part of Vimeo, Inc.)
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, New York, United States
enrichment.seo_title: Vimeo Events Review: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vimeo Events is Vimeo's virtual and hybrid events platform for livestreaming and webinars. Compare pricing, features, and alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Vimeo Events is a dedicated virtual and hybrid events product built by Vimeo, Inc., the New York City-based video hosting company originally founded in 2004 by Jake Lodwick and Zach Klein as a project of CollegeHumor. Vimeo launched the Events product in November 2021, extending its existing live-streaming capabilities (built partly on its 2017 acquisition of Livestream) into a full webinar and virtual conference platform, alongside its other 2021 acquisitions of interactive video company Wirewax and AI video creation tool Wibbitz. Vimeo itself became a standalone public company in May 2021 before being taken private in a $1.38 billion all-cash acquisition by Bending Spoons, announced in September 2025 and finalized in November 2025. Vimeo Events lets organizations run 4K multi-camera live streams, schedule pre-recorded content as simulated live broadcasts (simulive), and run 24/7 always-on channels, while offering audience engagement tools such as live polls, chat, and Q&A plus webinar registration workflows. It integrates with CRM platforms like HubSpot and Salesforce for lead capture and provides real-time analytics on attendance and viewer behavior. Pricing for the Advanced plan starts at $75 per month when billed annually, covering 10 seats and 7TB of storage, with a custom-priced Enterprise plan adding dedicated support and SSO/SCIM authentication for larger organizations. As part of the broader Vimeo platform, Vimeo Events competes with dedicated webinar and virtual event tools such as Zoom Events, ON24, and Hopin's successors, differentiating itself through Vimeo's video-hosting heritage, production quality tools, and integration with Vimeo's broader video library and analytics ecosystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Vimeo Events
paragraphs: Vimeo Events is a dedicated virtual and hybrid events product from Vimeo, Inc., designed for organizations that need to run livestreams, webinars, and online conferences with production-quality broadcasting tools.

Vimeo, the parent platform, was founded in 2004 by Jake Lodwick and Zach Klein as a video-sharing project within CollegeHumor and is headquartered in New York City. The Events product launched in November 2021, building on video and live-streaming technology Vimeo had accumulated through earlier acquisitions such as Livestream in 2017.

Vimeo Events extends the core Vimeo hosting platform with tools specifically for scheduled, interactive broadcasts, positioning it as a video-quality-focused alternative to standalone webinar platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Vimeo Events supports 4K live streaming with multi-camera switching, simulive broadcasting of pre-recorded content, and 24/7 always-on channels with automated scheduling, plus custom branding through logos, lower thirds, and graphic overlays.
Audience engagement tools include live polls, chat, and Q&A, along with a webinar registration system that sends automated confirmations and reminders to attendees.

Events are automatically recorded for on-demand playback afterward, and the platform can multi-stream to social channels while integrating with CRM tools like HubSpot and Salesforce for lead capture and follow-up.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Vimeo Events' Advanced plan starts at $75 per month when billed annually, including 10 seats and 7TB of storage.
An Enterprise plan is available with custom pricing for larger organizations, adding dedicated support and SSO/SCIM authentication.

features.description: Broadcast high-quality live video with switching between multiple camera feeds.

features.title: Simulive broadcasting
features.description: Schedule pre-recorded content to air as if it were live, with real-time chat alongside it.

features.title: 24/7 always-on channels
features.description: Run continuous automated broadcasting without manual scheduling for each session.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: Apply logos, lower-thirds, and graphic overlays to match event branding.

features.title: Webinar registration
features.description: Built-in registration pages with automated confirmation and reminder emails.

features.title: Live engagement tools
features.description: Polls, chat, and Q&A to keep attendees engaged during broadcasts.

features.title: CRM integrations
features.description: Connects with HubSpot and Salesforce for lead capture and post-event follow-up.

features.title: Automatic recording and analytics
features.description: Records events for on-demand replay and tracks real-time audience behavior.

pros: High production-quality live streaming compared to many webinar-only tools
Built-in CRM integrations for lead capture
Simulive and always-on channel options add scheduling flexibility
Backed by Vimeo's established video hosting infrastructure
Detailed real-time attendance and engagement analytics
cons: Pricing sits above some dedicated webinar-only competitors
Full feature set requires learning Vimeo's broader platform
Enterprise-level authentication features require custom pricing
Fewer virtual-event-specific extras (like expo halls) than specialized event platforms

faqs.question: What is Vimeo Events?
faqs.answer: Vimeo Events is Vimeo's product for hosting virtual and hybrid live streams, webinars, and online conferences with engagement and lead-capture tools.

faqs.question: How much does Vimeo Events cost?
faqs.answer: The Advanced plan starts at $75 per month when billed annually, covering 10 seats and 7TB of storage; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: When did Vimeo launch its Events product?
faqs.answer: Vimeo launched Vimeo Events in November 2021.

faqs.question: Who owns Vimeo?
faqs.answer: Vimeo was taken private by Bending Spoons in a deal announced in September 2025 and finalized in November 2025, after being a standalone public company since May 2021.

faqs.question: Does Vimeo Events support pre-recorded content?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vimeo Events supports simulive broadcasting, which schedules pre-recorded video to play as if it were a live event.

faqs.question: Does Vimeo Events integrate with CRM tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with HubSpot and Salesforce for lead capture during and after events.

faqs.question: Can Vimeo Events run continuous broadcasts?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports 24/7 always-on channels with automated scheduling.

faqs.question: Who is Vimeo Events built for?
faqs.answer: It is built for marketers, educators, and enterprises running webinars, product launches, and virtual or hybrid conferences.

enrichment.short_description: Vince is a free, open-source, self-hosted web analytics platform offering a privacy-friendly, cookie-free alternative to Google Analytics in a single lightweight binary.
enrichment.seo_title: Vince Analytics Review: Self-Hosted Google Analytics Alternative
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vince is a free, open-source, self-hosted web analytics tool and privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics. See features and setup details.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Vince is an open-source, self-hosted web analytics platform created by developer Geofrey Ernest, who spent roughly three years researching how to apply roaring bitmap indexing to web analytics event storage before releasing the project, which became publicly visible around late 2024 (including a Hacker News Show HN post). Positioned as a cost-effective, privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics, Vince ships as a single, dependency-free Go binary that administrators deploy on their own infrastructure, with automatic TLS support built in. It tracks standard web analytics data such as pageviews, custom events, goal conversions, outbound link clicks, file downloads, and 404 errors, while supporting unlimited websites and events within one self-hosted instance. Because it avoids cookies entirely, Vince is designed to comply with GDPR, CCPA, and the UK/EU's PECR regulation without requiring a cookie consent banner, similar in philosophy to other privacy-first analytics tools like Plausible Analytics and Umami, both of which the project's creator cited as inspiration and points of comparison when explaining why Vince was built. The software is licensed under AGPL-3.0 and distributed via GitHub (vinceanalytics/vince), where it continues to receive active updates (versioned releases such as v11.6 have been documented). Vince has no official managed cloud offering as of research; it is exclusively self-hosted, which sets it apart from competitors like Plausible and Umami that offer paid hosted versions alongside their open-source code. Because it is entirely free and open source with no vendor lock-in, Vince appeals most to developers and privacy-conscious organizations that want full control over their analytics data without paying a subscription fee to a third-party analytics provider.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Vince
paragraphs: Vince is an open-source, self-hosted web analytics tool built as a lightweight, privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics. It was created by developer Geofrey Ernest, who spent about three years researching how to apply roaring bitmap indexing to analytics event storage before releasing the project.

The tool emerged publicly around late 2024, positioned against existing privacy-focused analytics tools like Plausible Analytics, with the creator citing manageability and efficiency gaps in existing options as the motivation for building Vince.

Vince ships as a single, dependency-free binary written in Go, making deployment simple for developers who want to run their own analytics infrastructure rather than relying on a third-party SaaS provider.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Vince tracks pageviews, custom events, goal conversions, outbound link clicks, file downloads, and 404 errors without using cookies, helping sites comply with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR without a cookie consent banner.

It supports unlimited websites and events within a single self-hosted instance, includes automatic TLS support, and uses a lightweight tracking script under 1KB in size to minimize impact on page load speed.
The underlying storage engine uses roaring bitmap indexing to keep the analytics data compact and fast to query, even at meaningful scale, within a single binary deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Vince is completely free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license. There is no official paid or managed hosting tier; users deploy and run Vince entirely on their own infrastructure at no licensing cost.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Ships as one dependency-free Go binary, simplifying self-hosted setup.

features.title: Cookie-free tracking
features.description: Tracks visitors without cookies, avoiding the need for consent banners under most privacy laws.

features.title: Unlimited websites and events
features.description: No artificial limits on the number of sites or tracked events within one instance.

features.title: Automatic TLS
features.description: Built-in TLS certificate handling for secure deployments out of the box.

features.title: Custom event and goal tracking
features.description: Tracks custom events and conversion goals beyond basic pageviews.

features.title: Outbound link and download tracking
features.description: Automatically records outbound clicks and file downloads.

features.title: 404 error monitoring
features.description: Flags broken links and missing pages via 404 tracking.

features.title: Lightweight tracking script
features.description: Under 1KB script size to minimize impact on site performance.

pros: Completely free and open source
No cookies required, simplifying privacy compliance
Very lightweight single-binary deployment
Efficient roaring-bitmap storage engine for compact, fast analytics
Full data ownership since everything is self-hosted
cons: No official managed cloud hosting option available
Requires technical skill to deploy and maintain
Smaller community and ecosystem than Plausible or Umami
Limited third-party documentation and integrations so far

faqs.question: Is Vince free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vince is completely free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license, with no paid tiers.

faqs.question: Who created Vince?
faqs.answer: Vince was created by developer Geofrey Ernest after several years researching efficient analytics storage using roaring bitmap indexing.

faqs.question: Does Vince use cookies?
faqs.answer: No, Vince tracks visitors without cookies, which helps sites comply with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR without a cookie banner.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Vince?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vince is designed exclusively for self-hosting and ships as a single dependency-free binary.

faqs.question: Does Vince offer a managed cloud version?
faqs.answer: No, as of current research Vince does not offer an official managed hosting service; it is self-hosted only.

faqs.question: How does Vince compare to Plausible or Umami?
faqs.answer: Vince offers similar privacy-friendly, cookie-free analytics but is built around a different, highly efficient storage engine and is exclusively self-hosted.

faqs.question: What license does Vince use?
faqs.answer: Vince is licensed under AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: Does Vince support unlimited websites?
faqs.answer: Yes, a single Vince instance can track unlimited websites and events.

use_cases.description: Tracking site traffic without cookies to simplify GDPR and CCPA compliance.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted infrastructure control
use_cases.description: Running analytics entirely on owned infrastructure instead of a third-party SaaS.

use_cases.title: Lightweight analytics for multiple sites
use_cases.description: Tracking many websites from a single low-resource analytics instance.
use_cases.audience: Agencies and indie developers managing several sites

enrichment.short_description: Visme is an AI-powered visual content platform for creating presentations, infographics, reports, and branded documents through templates and design tools.
enrichment.seo_title: Visme Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Visme is an AI-powered design tool for presentations, infographics, and branded content. Compare pricing, features, and alternatives like Canva.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Visme is an AI-powered visual content creation platform founded by Payman Taei, who began building it around 2012-2013 as a side project alongside his existing Flash-based web design agency in Rockville, Maryland; he continued running the agency alongside Visme from 2013 to 2018, reinvesting agency profits into the product, and the company became profitable around 2015 without ever raising outside venture funding, remaining fully bootstrapped. Visme now reports over 35 million users and roughly $15 million in annual recurring revenue with a team of under 100 employees, still headquartered in Rockville, Maryland. The platform lets business teams design presentations, infographics, reports, proposals, social media graphics, and other branded visual content using an AI Designer that turns text prompts into ready-made layouts, a large template library spanning 50-plus content types, interactive data visualization tools, a Brand Wizard for automated brand-consistent design, and real-time team collaboration features. Visme's pricing follows a freemium model: a free Basic plan offers unlimited projects with limited templates and assets; a Starter plan costs about $12.25 per month billed annually ($147/year); a Pro plan costs about $24.75 per month billed annually ($297/year) and adds video/GIF/PPTX exports, brand kits, and analytics; and a custom-priced Enterprise plan (minimum 10 users) adds SSO, dedicated support, and advanced security. Discounted Student and Educator plans are also available. Visme competes most directly with Canva and Venngage in the visual design and infographic space, differentiating through deeper data visualization and interactive content features (like clickable hotspots and animations) aimed more at business reporting and presentations than social-media-first design.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Visme
paragraphs: Visme is an AI-powered content creation platform that helps business teams design and manage branded presentations, infographics, reports, proposals, and other visual content at scale.

The company was founded by Payman Taei, who started building Visme around 2012-2013 as a side project alongside his existing web design agency, reinvesting agency revenue into the product until it became profitable around 2015. Visme has remained fully bootstrapped, never raising outside venture capital.
Headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, Visme now serves more than 35 million users globally and generates roughly $15 million in annual recurring revenue with a lean team of fewer than 100 employees.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Visme's AI Designer converts text prompts into ready-to-use design layouts, while its template library spans more than 50 content types, from presentations to social graphics to infographics.

The platform includes interactive data visualization with charts and graphs, a Brand Wizard for automatically applying brand colors, fonts, and logos across content, and interactivity features like clickable hotspots, animations, and embedded links.
Real-time collaboration tools let teams comment, review, and co-edit designs, and a content calendar helps plan and schedule social media posts alongside other visual content.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Visme offers a free Basic plan with unlimited projects but limited templates and assets. The Starter plan costs about $12.25 per month billed annually ($147/year), and the Pro plan costs about $24.75 per month billed annually ($297/year), adding video and PPTX exports, brand kits, and analytics.

An Enterprise plan with custom pricing (minimum 10 users) adds SSO, enhanced security, and a dedicated customer success manager, while discounted Student ($48/year) and Educator ($96/year) plans are also available.

features.description: Thousands of templates across more than 50 content types, from presentations to infographics.

features.title: Data visualization
features.description: Interactive charts, graphs, and data widgets for turning statistics into visuals.

features.title: Brand Wizard
features.description: Automatically applies brand colors, fonts, and logos across new designs.

features.title: Interactivity tools
features.description: Clickable hotspots, animations, and embeds for interactive presentations and documents.

features.title: Content calendar
features.description: Plans and schedules social media and content publishing from within Visme.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Commenting and co-editing tools for teams working on shared designs.

features.title: Large asset library
features.description: Millions of stock photos, icons, videos, and 3D graphics available in-app.

pros: Strong data visualization and interactive content features beyond basic design tools
Generous free plan with unlimited projects
Bootstrapped, profitable company with a long track record
Brand Wizard simplifies consistent brand application across content
Wide range of content types beyond just social graphics
cons: Free plan restricts access to premium templates and export formats
Enterprise plan requires a 10-user minimum, less suited to very small teams
Learning curve for advanced interactivity and data visualization features
Smaller template ecosystem than Canva's much larger user-generated marketplace

faqs.question: Is Visme free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Visme offers a free Basic plan with unlimited projects, though it has limited templates, assets, and export options.

faqs.question: How much does Visme cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is about $12.25 per month billed annually, and the Pro plan is about $24.75 per month billed annually; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Who founded Visme?
faqs.answer: Visme was founded by Payman Taei, who built it starting around 2012-2013 as a side project of his web design agency.

faqs.question: Is Visme a publicly funded or venture-backed company?
faqs.answer: No, Visme is fully bootstrapped and has never raised outside venture capital funding.

faqs.question: Where is Visme headquartered?
faqs.answer: Visme is headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, United States.

faqs.question: How does Visme compare to Canva?
faqs.answer: Visme offers deeper data visualization, interactivity, and brand management tools aimed at business reporting, while Canva is broader and more social-media-design focused.

faqs.question: Does Visme offer discounts for students and educators?
faqs.answer: Yes, Visme offers a Student plan at $48 per year and an Educator plan at $96 per year.

faqs.question: Can teams collaborate in Visme?
faqs.answer: Yes, Visme supports real-time collaboration with commenting and co-editing on shared projects.
